studies.title: Multicenter randomized controlled trial (RCT) to demonstrate the positive care effects of the medical device companion® shoulder in adult patients with conservatively managed shoulder lesions with subacromial pain
studies.brief_summary: The COMPASS study is a randomized, controlled, two-arm, multicenter, open-label, superiority study to demonstrate positive care effects of the DiGA companion® shoulder in conservatively treated adult patients with shoulder lesions with subacromial pain. In total, n=270 patients will be randomized (1:1) into an intervention group and a control group. The intervention group receives the DiGA companion® shoulder, while the control group receives the standard of care for 90 days, respectively. The study investigates the efficacy and safety of the DiGA in conservatively treated adult patients with shoulder lesions with subacromial pain.
